title: Add check 5 to verify.py (significant shape overlap)
description: Extend pypptx/ops/verify.py with check 5 (significant shape overlap). Iterate over all pairs of shapes on each slide, skipping any shape whose width or height is below 50,000 EMU. Compute the intersection rectangle of each pair. Report a WARNING when the intersection area exceeds 0.3 sq inches AND exceeds 20% of the smaller shape's area AND neither shape fully contains the other.
acceptance_criteria: Check 5 reports WARNING when two non-tiny shapes overlap significantly (>0.3 sq in and >20% of smaller shape)
Check 5 does not trigger when one shape fully contains the other
Check 5 does not trigger when either shape is smaller than 50,000 EMU in width or height
Check 5 does not trigger when overlap area is below 0.3 sq inches
Check 5 does not trigger when overlap is below 20% of the smaller shape's area
Check 5 does not trigger on non-overlapping shapes

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
